title: Rubus procerus microgene occiduus
intro:
I couldn't find any information on a plant called Rubus procerus microgene occiduus. However, I can provide information on a similar plant, Rubus occidentalis, also known as American black raspberry.

Rubus occidentalis is a type of bramble or thorny shrub, belonging to the Rubus genus of flowering plants. It is known for its edible fruits, which have various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to protect against chronic diseases such as cancer, cardiovascular disease, and cognitive decline.

The bioactive constituents of Rubus occidentalis include anthocyanins, ellagic acid, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have anti-cancer, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. These constituents may also contribute to the plant's potential health benefits.

Rubus occidentalis is commonly used in herbal preparations, including jams, jellies, and teas, as well as in supplement forms such as capsules and extracts.
